name: A3 Association for Advancing Automation
description: This association has not added a description.
address: 900 Victors Way, Suite 140, Ann Arbor, MI, 48108, United States

name: AER Automation
description:
AER Automation is the leading Spanish association of Robotics and Automation, a
non-profit organization born in 1985. A founding member of the International Federation
of Robotics (IFR, 1987), and member of the CEOE (Spanish Confederation of Employers’
Organizations). With more than 100 members, AER Automation covers the whole value
chain of automation, from the suppliers to end users, including distributors, integrators,
technological centers, startups and education institutions. Its mission is to promote
competitiveness of the Spanish industry -especially SMEs – through robotics and automation
technologies, as well as to guarantee fluid access for qualified talent relating to
industry 4.0.
address: Ceramistes, 2 Parc Tecnològic del Vallès, 08290, Cerdanyola del Vallés, Barcelona, Spain

name: FFC Robotique
description: FFC Robotique is the official federation of the French regional robotics clusters. Our members are around 600 robotics companies. We have expert companies in either industrial robotics or service robotics (mobile autonomous robots, surgical robot, farming robot, swimming pool cleaning robot, Drones, submarine robots ...) We are a private non profit organization. We are able to organize consortium of companies and laboratories to developp new kind of robots for new needs or we can just provide you the contact with the right company to provide you an existing standard solution when it exists. We are open to international partnership as far as it help to developp the robotics.

name: High Tech NL cluster Robotics
description: High Tech NL cluster Robotics (formerly known as Holland Robotics) goal is to strengthen the Dutch robotics sector by stimulating innovation, adaptation and implementation of robots in for example healthcare, agriculture and SME’s. We are the bridge between science, knowledge institutes, industry and robotics suppliers and have a leading position in The Netherlands in facilitating projects with government or EU funding. By combining our forces we aim to strengthen our international competitive position.
address: High Tech Campus 27, 5656 AE, Eindhoven, Netherlands

name: NFEA - The Norwegian Society of Electric and Automatic Control
description:
NFEA (Norwegian Society of Electric and Automatic Control) was established on 1st January 2018, as a merging of former Ifea (Industriens forening for elektro og automatisering) and former NFA (Norsk Forening for Automatisering).

Administration
The NFEA office has four employees, and is situated in Kristiansand in the South of Norway.

Board
The NFEA board consists of businessmen and -women who play an important role in defining the organisation’s strategy. Students are also represented in the NFEA board, with one member.

Mission and Strategy
NFEA Slogan:
Passionately concerned about the competence of the future
– through learning, sharing and networking.

NFEA’s values:
Provident – we understand the technology, and are at the forefront of development
Engaging – our activities engages through being inspiring and relevant
Generosity – we share competence and experience

Expert Panel
NFEA has a total of 13 experts connected. These experts are either experts within their industry, or within a specific technology area. For the next years we have experts within industries like oil&gas, infrastucture and medicin, and within tecnologies like digital transformation, robotics, cyber security and electical safety. See the norwegian presentation for a total overview.

Cooperation
NFEA will cooperate with relevant actors to be able to offer an interesting and relevant portfolio of activities (courses, seminars, conferences). Both national and international partners are being evaluated consecutively.
IFAC (International Federation of Automatic Control)
NFEA is IFAC NMO (National Member Organisation). This means that NFEA represents Norway in IFAC and vice versa. IFAC webpage is found here.
IFAC arranges a lot of global activites within Automatic control. They also have a number of gobal, technical committees where Norway also have representatives.
